page: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the system includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided by a local 480 GB NVMe SSD. The server does not include a GPU.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, falls into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with PassMark CPU and memory scores, also rank in the average tier. For language model tasks, text generation with a small 135M model achieves top-tier speeds, whereas prompt processing with a medium 7B model is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

The shared CPU allocation presents a resource-sharing tradeoff, which may impact multi-threaded performance consistency compared to dedicated instances. However, the inclusion of a 480 GB local NVMe SSD and 32.0 GB of memory provides a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads. This server is configured for workloads such as static web serving, redis caching, database operations, and single-threaded processing tasks that benefit from its high single-core performance.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s is an x86_64 server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 8 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Turin processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 480 GB local NVMe SSD. While multi-core benchmarks and memory bandwidth rank in the average tier, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs, but the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local NVMe storage offer solid resource density. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, database operations, and single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s is an x86_64 server with 8 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s features 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD for general-purpose and single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A shared-core x86_64 server featuring AMD EPYC processors, local NVMe storage, and top-tier single-threaded performance for balanced workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory and 480 GB local NVMe SSD
